question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 removes its Loopback0 (192.0.2.2/32) from OSPF Area 0. R3 and R4
no longer learn an OSPF route to 192.0.2.2. R4's iBGP session to R2
is loopback-peered; with the route gone, R4 cannot reach 192.0.2.2 →
the TCP session for iBGP drops. PE-A and PE-B stop exchanging routes
even though the data-plane link R2-R3-R4 still works for transit
between non-loopback IPs.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), Loopback0 (192.0.2.2/32) was removed from OSPF area 0, so R3 and R4 no longer learn an OSPF route to 192.0.2.2.
R4's iBGP session to R2 is loopback-peered, so once the route to 192.0.2.2 disappears the TCP session can no longer be reached and the iBGP session cannot stay established; PE-A and PE-B stop exchanging routes even though transit between non-loopback IPs still works.
Fix: re-advertise R2's Loopback0 into OSPF area 0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.2.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R2, R4 (2 instances):
- R2: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
no interface Loopback0

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface Loopback0
 passive enable

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:07:36.022 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:07:36.021 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.2 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:07:36.023 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:07:36.021 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
